title: Accessible Puzzle Interface and Multichannel Feedback · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed puzzle review reconstructs accessible puzzle interface and multichannel feedback from revision, configuration, seed, topology, frontier, predecessor map, state transition, draw record, accessibility report, profile, artifact, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Provide a synchronized structured-text grid, route or step list, coordinates, and status that convey essential puzzle state.
Communicate wall, focus, visited, frontier, route, success, and error through redundant text, shape, pattern, position, and optional sound.
Provide keyboard navigation, remapping, visible focus, large targets, clear labels, no traps, and announced state changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Text Alternative · changed case · Review Text Alternative
steps.instruction:
review case — A review puzzle team handles case 6031 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. stimulus.title: Accessibility Evidence case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review puzzle team handles case 5317 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, scaling, contrast, screen-reader, switch-like input, and disability-led playtest evidence.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: WCAG-oriented code inspection alone proves the desktop puzzle is accessible.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, scaling, contrast, screen-reader, switch-like input, and disability-led playtest evidence. Artifact under review: keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, solve one convenient seeded maze, omit revision, coordinate and wall invariants, topology oracle, frontier trace, changed algorithm or heuristic, callback ownership, keyboard and structured-text access, native Tk evidence, failure, recovery, and package transfer, then accept a screenshot as maze proof. This does not satisfy Accessibility Evidence for keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface.
The record names the assumption, applies Accessibility Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Accessibility Evidence record says keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Accessibility Evidence.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Accessibility Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Accessibility Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review puzzle team handles case 3698 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, scaling, contrast, screen-reader, switch-like input, and disability-led playtest evidence.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Accessibility Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Accessibility Evidence through the concrete keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Accessibility Evidence, keep Accessibility Evidence connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, scaling, contrast, screen-reader, switch-like input, and disability-led playtest evidence.
